top_stories.title: Yields on government debt end lower
top_stories.summary: YIELDS on government securities (GS) closed mostly lower last week as traders stayed cautious over developments in the Middle East conflict while pricing in a potential rate hike from the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) as early as Thursday. GS yields, which move opposite to prices, went down by an average of 3.41...

top_stories.title: Poll: BSP to kick off tightening cycle as high oil prices bite
top_stories.summary: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) is widely expected to raise interest rates this week for the first time in more than two years, a step that would position the Philippines among the first Asian economies to pivot toward tighter monetary policy as surging oil prices fuel a fresh wave of inflation. top_stories.title: Higher prices or slower growth? BSP faces policy tug-of-war
top_stories.summary: Private sector economists are split over whether the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) will raise interest rates this week, as the need to anchor inflation expectations and defend the peso battles concerns over the fragile economic recovery. top_stories.title: BPI posts modest Q1 profit rise to P16.9B
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines – Bank of the Philippine Islands (BPI) reported a net income of P16.9 billion in the first quarter, up 1.7 percent, as loan expansion, wider margins and stronger fee income lifted earnings despite rising costs. top_stories.title: BSP’s rules on loan, fees must be followed—solon
top_stories.summary: SENATOR Joel Villanueva called as timely the recent guidance by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) for banks to waive digital fund transfer fees and to provide loan relief measures for borrowers hit by the ongoing energy crisis.
